commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Manual maintainer/security review remains because the patch deliberately omits legacy binding recovery or fail-closed behavior for saved records without agentId.; Needs attention: The patch is security-boundary relevant and still leaves existing binding records without agentId on the older fallback policy path.; Review finding: [P1] Handle bindings that lack agentId — extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding... (sha=ff2ba59a5537759e5f9b5f6856502b478f86e027)
commands.review_summary:
The PR persists a Codex app-server binding agent id, passes it through `/codex bind`, native execution guard, app-server runtime policy, bound turns, and missing-thread recovery, with focused tests.

PR surface: Source +21, Tests +284. Total +305 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: use an existing app-server binding record whose data lacks `agentId`, then send a no-session inbound bound turn for a non-default bound agent with stricter exec policy; the resolver falls back to default/first-agent policy.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual maintainer/security review remains because the patch deliberately omits legacy binding recovery or fail-closed behavior for saved records without `agentId`.

target.title: fix(codex): honor bound agent exec host policy
target.body:
## Summary
- store the binding agent id for newly created Codex app-server conversation bindings
- pass the stored binding agent id into the Codex native execution guard
- carry the same binding agent id into app-server runtime policy resolution for bind, bound-turn, and missing-thread recovery paths
- block or reshape app-server execution according to the bound agent's policy even when an inbound channel event has no session key

## What Changed
- `/codex bind` now passes the current session agent id into `startCodexConversationThread`.
- Codex app-server conversation binding data now persists optional `agentId` next to `sessionFile`, `workspaceDir`, and `agentDir`.
- Bound app-server inbound turns now pass `data.agentId` to the native execution guard, so agent-scoped `tools.exec.host=node` policy is checked even when the inbound event has no session key.
- App-server thread attach/create, later bound turns, and missing-thread recovery now pass the same bound `agentId` into runtime policy resolution, so approval and sandbox policy also come from the bound agent.
- Command and conversation-binding tests cover the new argument, no-session bound-turn blocks, agent-scoped app-server approval policy, and missing-thread recovery.

## Scope
This intentionally applies to newly created Codex app-server conversation bindings. Existing saved bindings that predate `data.agentId` can keep the previous fallback behavior until the conversation is detached and rebound.

That keeps this PR narrow: it does not add migration or legacy binding inference logic.

## Flow
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph before["Before this PR"]
    direction TB
    b1["Discord runs /codex bind"] --> b2["Binding stores sessionFile, workspaceDir, agentDir"]
    b2 --> b3["Bound agent id is not stored, example: discord-bot-a"]
    b3 --> b4["Later Discord message resumes the bound Codex app-server session"]
    b4 --> b5["Inbound event has no session key"]
    b5 --> b6["Guard cannot check discord-bot-a"]
    b6 --> b7["Runtime policy also lacks discord-bot-a"]
    b7 --> b8["Policy may fall back to default agent"]
    b8 --> b9["Codex app-server may use the wrong exec, approval, or sandbox policy"]
  end

  subgraph after["After this PR, for new bindings"]
    direction TB
    a1["Discord runs /codex bind"] --> a2["Binding stores sessionFile, workspaceDir, agentDir, agentId"]
    a2 --> a3["Stored agent id example: discord-bot-a"]
    a3 --> a4["Later Discord message resumes the bound Codex app-server session"]
    a4 --> a5["Inbound event has no session key"]
    a5 --> a6["Guard checks discord-bot-a"]
    a6 --> a7{"discord-bot-a tools.exec.host"}
    a7 -- "node" --> a8["Block native Codex app-server execution"]
    a7 -- "auto, sandbox, or gateway" --> a9["Runtime policy also checks discord-bot-a"]
    a9 --> a10["Attach, create, turn/start, and recovery use bound-agent policy"]
  end
```

## Proof
| Evidence | Claim Proved | How To Check |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Net diff: 6 Codex files, `+306/-1`, no branch-only churn | Current PR only changes binding data, bind command plumbing, guard/runtime policy input, and focused tests | `git diff --stat b02b05d13455bf251ddbcbe17b476b81fa7801b0...ff2ba59a5537759e5f9b5f6856502b478f86e027` |
| Focused local Vitest red/green | Before the second commit, the new tests failed because bind, bound-turn, and recovery policy used default-agent policy; after the fix, those paths use the stored binding agent | `PATH=/Users/jmerhi/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v22.21.0/installation/bin:$PATH PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/Users/jmerhi/repos/claw-working-space/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` |
| GitHub checks on `ff2ba59a5537759e5f9b5f6856502b478f86e027` | Full PR validation is rerunning after the rebase and reviewer-fix commit | `gh pr checks 86360 --repo openclaw/openclaw` |
| Mermaid validation | Before/after flow renders through Mermaid CLI before posting | `mmdc -i /tmp/pr86360-final-2.mmd -o /tmp/pr86360-final-2.svg` |

## How To Test
```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:extensions
pnpm tsgo:test:extensions
```

## Verification
```text
python3 /Users/jmerhi/repos/AGENT.MDs/skills/pr-proof-pack/scripts/pr_net_diff.py --markdown --proof-plan
pass: 6 files changed, 306 insertions, 1 deletion; no branch-only churn

PATH=/Users/jmerhi/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v22.21.0/installation/bin:$PATH PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/Users/jmerhi/repos/claw-working-space/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts -- --reporter=dot
pass: 2 files, 149 tests

mmdc -i /tmp/pr86360-final-2.mmd -o /tmp/pr86360-final-2.svg
pass

gh pr checks 86360 --repo openclaw/openclaw
pending on ff2ba59a5537759e5f9b5f6856502b478f86e027 after push
```
